content:
I am an **AI Engineer** working on this project with the following core skills:

- **LLM Engineering**: Integrating and optimizing Anthropic Claude (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus); structured outputs, tool use, prompt chaining, context window management
- **Prompt Engineering**: System prompt design, few-shot prompting, chain-of-thought, token optimization, chunked parallel LLM calls
- **Machine Learning**: AutoML pipelines, time-series forecasting (SARIMA, XGBoost, moving avg, exp smoothing), cross-validat

- FastAPI + asyncpg backend for restaurant inventory management
- Path: /Users/bharadwajeyunni/Documents/Automl
- DB: postgresql://bharadwajeyunni@/ownerly?host=/tmp (Unix socket, homebrew postgres)
- Schema file: ownerly.sql
- GitHub: https://github.com/ownerly25/datagatheringserv

- Claude's Bash tool CANNOT start the server reliably due to sandbox restrictions on postgres Unix socket
- User must start server from their own terminal: `python -m uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000`
- Correct venv: .venv-1 (Python 3.13, has fastapi + asyncpg + anthropic)
- Do NOT try to start server via background Bash tasks - it will fail

```bash
/opt/homebrew/bin/psql -h /tmp -p 5432 -U postgres -c "CREATE ROLE bharadwajeyunni WITH LOGIN SUPERUSER;"
/opt/homebrew/bin/psql -h /tmp -p 5432 -U postgres -c "CREATE DATABASE ownerly OWNER bharadwajeyunni;"
/opt/homebrew/bin/psql -h /tmp -p 5432 -U bharadwajeyunni -d ownerly -f /Users/bharadwajeyunni/Documents/Automl/ownerly.sql
```

- Uses Anthropic Claude Haiku (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001) via API
- API key in .env as ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
- detect_data_type: LLM reads first 2000 chars → classifies as inventory/menu/staff/financial/unknown (30s timeout, 2 retries)
- extract_records: filters date-pattern lines, deduplicates, chunks 60 lines at a time, parallel via asyncio.gather (120s timeout, 2 retries)
- Philosophy: NO hardcoded rules. LLM reads raw file text and extracts everything

- app/services/llm_service.py - LLM service (Anthropic AsyncAnthropic client)
- app/services/extraction.py - File extraction (Excel/CSV/PDF/Image via pytesseract)
- app/services/cleaning.py - Cleans extracted data, deduplicates by (item_name.lower(), date), inserts to inventory_history
- app/services/ml_service.py - AutoML (moving avg, exp smoothing, SARIMA, XGBoost + time-series CV)
- app/services/scheduler.py - Background weekly ML training via asyncio loop (checks hourly)
- app/config.py - Se

- uploads, extractions, inventory_history, inventory_predictions
- model_registry, ml_locks, owner_preferences, api_keys, upload_errors

- business_id: a3f7b2c1-4d8e-4f9a-b6c2-1e5d7f3a8b4c
- X-API-Key: sk-test-ownerly-9f4a2b8c3d1e7f6a

- Groups by (item_name, unit) when unit column has values → separate prediction per unit variant
- 41 items × ~4-5 unit variants = ~181 predictions per training run
- simple_average wins most (insufficient data per unit variant with only 8 weeks)

Done: rate limiting, API key auth, health check, request logging, DB pooling,
      Anthropic retry+timeout, Alembic, Dockerfile+.dockerignore, CORS middleware,
      file size/type/empty checks, ML DB lock for crash recovery

After server chosen: HTTPS, real CORS domain, S3 file storage, gunicorn workers,
                     log shipping, scheduler dedup (multi-container)

File upload → validate (type/size/empty) → save to disk → background task:
  extract to markdown → LLM detect type → LLM extract records (chunked parallel)
  → deduplicate → insert to inventory_history → delete file from disk

- .venv-1 (Python 3.13) - CORRECT venv, has anthropic + asyncpg + fastapi
- .venv-ownerly (Python 3.11) - missing anthropic
- .venv (Python 3.9) - missing asyncpg and anthropic

- Files: app/services/promotion_service.py, app/routers/promotions.py
- Scheduler triggers ML + promotions together (same prediction_day from owner_preferences)
- Partially fixed: week_start, max_tokens 2048, sonnet model, open-ended suggestions
- BIG REWRITE PENDING — see [Promotions Rewrite Plan](project_promotions_rewrite.md)

name: Promotions Rewrite Plan
description: Full plan for rewriting the promotion service to work for all restaurant types with smart deals, vibe context, and owner-safe margins
type: project
---
Everything below is agreed and ready to implement in one agent run.

- `week_start` uses owner's `prediction_day` (not Monday hardcoded)
- Both DB queries (MAX sale_date + owner_preferences) in one connection
- `max_tokens` 1024 → 2048
- Model switched to `claude-sonnet-4-6` for promotion generation
- Upsell + payment suggestions are open-ended (not hardcoded templates)

```json
{
  "type": "low_seller",
  "promo_name": "Sweet Rescue",
  "items": ["Rasgulla"],
  "original_price": 4.63,
  "promo_price": 3.70,
  "discount_pct": 20,
  "suggestion": "Our most underrated dessert — try it once, it becomes your regular",
  "best_days": "Tuesday and Wednesday",
  "reason": "Slowest item, 20% off drives trial"
}
```

- Option B: Python sends shortlists, LLM picks final items + exact discount + writes copy
- No hardcoded cuisine assumptions anywhere in Python
- Sonnet decides combos using food intelligence (knows Idly + Chicken Curry is bad)
- Discount ranges computed from food_cost_pct floor, not fixed percentages
- Vibe presets (not free text) so owner picks from UI, LLM gets controlled quality context
- Day-of-week timing from sales data (no hourly data in schema — sale_time not stored)

### Roles
- `superadmin` — Your team. Creates tenants (wear product companies), uploads Excel per tenant.
- `admin` — Tenant admin (e.g. Artha Weartech). Views their data, manages their users.
- `user` — Mining companies (e.g. BHP). Self-registers, picks a tenant at registration, uses calculator.

### Key Flow
1. Superadmin creates tenant (org) + tenant admin account via /superadmin page
2. Superadmin uploads Excel data for that tenant
3. Mining company (user) goes to /register, fills details +

- [ ] Re-enable work email block in `/register` (currently commented out for testing)
- [ ] Set real `FRONTEND_URL` in `.env` (currently localhost:3000)
- [ ] Test forgot password flow with real email end to end
- [ ] Set up systemd to keep backend running on server
- [ ] Set up server (find/configure production server)
- [ ] Write backend unit tests (pytest)

- [ ] Dashboard for admin — charts: user count, active/expired users, data uploaded
- [ ] Export TCU comparison results as PDF or Excel
- [ ] Email user 7 days before access expires
- [ ] User profile page — user sees their own details and expiry date
- [ ] Superadmin can see all users across all tenants
- [ ] Audit logs — track logins, approvals, uploads
- [ ] Re-send verification email button (if user didn't get it)
- [ ] Search/filter in users table (for admins with many users)
- [ ] Calculat

- [ ] Enable HTTPS on server
- [ ] Add password strength rules (min 8 chars, number + special char)
- [ ] Consider refresh tokens for JWT auto-renewal

- [x] 3-tier multi-tenant architecture (superadmin, admin, user)
- [x] JWT auth with role-based access
- [x] Superadmin creates tenants, sends welcome email
- [x] Email verification on registration
- [x] User approval flow with access days
- [x] Block personal emails (gmail, yahoo etc.) — commented out for testing
- [x] Forgot password / reset password
- [x] Admin can approve/reject/edit days for users
- [x] Email notifications for all key actions (background tasks)
- [x] Rate limiting on login/

- [Resume - No Professional Summary](feedback_resume_no_summary.md) — user wants one-page resume; never suggest adding a summary/objective section
- [No fabricated metrics or numbers](feedback_no_fabricated_numbers.md) — never invent numbers/percentages in resume bullets; only use figures already in original text

name: No fabricated metrics or numbers
description: Never invent or estimate numbers/metrics when rewriting resume bullets
type: feedback
originSessionId: da5ae2ab-352f-49c2-b0e0-6ea5c92e1afc
---
Do not write numbers, percentages, or metrics into resume bullets unless they already exist in the original text.

**Why:** Resume claims must be truthful; made-up figures can be challenged in interviews and damage credibility.

**How to apply:** When rewriting bullets, preserve existing numbers exactly

name: Resume - No Professional Summary
description: User prefers one-page resume and explicitly does not want a professional summary added
type: feedback
originSessionId: da5ae2ab-352f-49c2-b0e0-6ea5c92e1afc
---
Do not suggest adding a Professional Summary or Objective section to this user's resume.

**Why:** User wants to keep resume to one page — a summary would push it over.

**How to apply:** Skip summary recommendations for all resume advice. Focus ATS improvements on Skills section keyword
- [No backend changes](feedback_no_backend_changes.md) — Never edit Automl/ or identityserv/ — backend is owned by a separate developer

name: No backend changes
description: Do not modify backend Python files — backend is owned by a separate developer
type: feedback
originSessionId: f74e4cbf-fd4c-4c00-b7e3-17182c8966ad
---
Never edit files in `/Users/bharadwajeyunni/Documents/identityserv/`. The identity service is maintained by a separate developer/friend.

The Automl backend (`/Users/bharadwajeyunni/Documents/Automl/`) CAN be edited — the user explicitly gave permission on 2026-05-11.

**Why:** Identity service is owned by a f
File unknown was edited 187 times this session — likely a hot path worth monitoring.
